title: SEC Litigation Release / SEC v. Michael R. Milken et al. — settlement, penalties, and lifetime industry bar
bias_or_interest: Regulator of record; authoritative on the civil settlement, the monetary penalties, and the permanent securities-industry bar.
relevant_locations: Permanent bar from association with any broker, dealer, investment adviser, or investment company (SEC lifetime industry ban)
Disgorgement/penalty figures tied to the ~$600M total resolution
Bar not lifted by any later clemency
quotation_note: U.S. government work, public domain; attribute as the SEC. Confirm the exact release text and figures on sec.gov before publish.

title: United States v. Michael R. Milken — 1989 indictment (98 counts, incl. RICO) and April 1990 guilty plea to six felony counts (S.D.N.Y.)
organization: U.S. Department of Justice / U.S. Attorney, Southern District of New York
bias_or_interest: Prosecuting authority / court record; authoritative on the count of charges, the RICO charge, and the six counts of the guilty plea.
relevant_locations: March 1989: 98-count indictment including RICO racketeering, securities fraud, mail fraud, insider trading, stock parking, tax
April 24, 1990: guilty plea to SIX felony counts (securities/reporting violations, conspiracy, mail fraud, tax)
RICO charge and the remaining 92 counts dropped; no trial
Sentence of 10 years (later reduced)
quotation_note: Cite as the indictment / plea allocution / court record. Distinguish CHARGED vs PLEADED precisely; charges are accusations, not findings.
rights_note: Court records / U.S. govt work; paraphrase, short attributed quotes. Confirm exact counts and dates against the docket before publish.
notes: PRIMARY court/DOJ record. CRITICAL for the CHARGED-vs-PLEADED wording lock. Durable hash PENDING.

title: Statement from the Press Secretary Regarding Executive Grants of Clemency (Michael Milken pardon)
organization: The White House (Office of the Press Secretary)
bias_or_interest: Issuing authority for the pardon; advocacy framing (praises philanthropy, calls offenses 'novel', lists supporters). Note: the statement says 'pleaded guilty in 1989' — imprecise; the plea was April 1990.
relevant_locations: February 18, 2020: full pardon granted to Michael Milken
Statement praises philanthropy; characterizes offenses as technical/regulatory 'novel' charges
Lists prominent supporters who advocated for clemency
(Imprecision: 'pleaded guilty in 1989' — actual plea April 1990)
quotation_note: Attribute every characterization to the White House statement; do NOT adopt its 'novel offenses' framing as fact. A pardon is clemency, not innocence.
rights_note: Public domain (U.S. govt, archived). Capture durable hash from the archived page before publish.
notes: PRIMARY for the 2020 pardon + its framing/supporters. Pardon != exoneration; plea stands; SEC ban not lifted. Durable hash PENDING.
